intro: Triticum thaoudar, also known as Moroccan wheat or Spelt, is an ancient species of wheat that is closely related to emmer wheat and is considered to be a descendant of one of the earliest cultivated wheat species. The health benefits of Triticum thaoudar include its potential to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and provide essential nutrients such as fiber, iron, and B vitamins. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other antioxidant compounds that contribute to its health-promoting properties. Triticum thaoudar is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being incorporated into traditional breads and baked goods.
